def modify_features(df_input) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """
    Thought 1: Combining the uniformity of cell size and cell shape with clump thickness into a single metric can provide an overall measure of abnormality in cellular structure. This could help differentiate between benign and malignant cases.
    New Feature 1: structural_abnormality_index | structural_abnormality_index = (Cell_Size_Uniformity + Cell_Shape_Uniformity + Clump_Thickness) / 3
    """
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    """
    Thought 1: Combining the uniformity of cell size and cell shape with clump thickness into a single metric can provide an overall measure of abnormality in cellular structure. This could help differentiate between benign and malignant cases.
    Thought 2: The ratio of abnormal cell features to total features can be an important indicator of malignant cells. We calculate two new features, abnormal_cell_ratio and abnormal_cell_concentration.
    New Feature 1: structural_abnormality_index | structural_abnormality_index = (Cell_Size_Uniformity + Cell_Shape_Uniformity + Clump_Thickness) / 3
    New Feature 2: abnormal_cell_ratio | abnormal_cell_ratio = Count of (Clump_Thickness > 5 or Cell_Size_Uniformity > 5 or Cell_Shape_Uniformity > 5 or Bland_Chromatin > 5 or Normal_Nucleoli > 5) / Total Number of Features
    New Feature 3: abnormal_cell_concentration | abnormal_cell_concentration = Count of (Bare_Nuclei > 5) / Count of (Mitoses > 5)
    
    :param df_input: DataFrame containing the input features
    :return: DataFrame with modified features
    """
    df_output = df_input.copy()

    # Calculate the structural abnormality index
    df_output['structural_abnormality_index'] = (
        df_output['Cell_Size_Uniformity'] + df_output['Cell_Shape_Uniformity'] + df_output['Clump_Thickness']
    ) / 3

    # Calculate the abnormal cell ratio
    abnormal_features = df_output[
        (df_output['Clump_Thickness'] > 5) | (df_output['Cell_Size_Uniformity'] > 5) | 
        (df_output['Cell_Shape_Uniformity'] > 5) | (df_output['Bland_Chromatin'] > 5) | 
        (df_output['Normal_Nucleoli'] > 5)
    ]
    df_output['abnormal_cell_ratio'] = len(abnormal_features) / len(df_output)

    # Calculate the abnormal cell concentration
    df_output['abnormal_cell_concentration'] = (
        df_output['Bare_Nuclei'] / df_output['Mitoses']
    ).where(df_output['Mitoses'] > 5, np.nan)

    # Drop redundant features
    df
